game_name: Shining Force: The Legacy of Great Intention

design_philosophy.core_loop: Turn-based tactical grid combat with character recruitment, leveling, and class progression interspersed with exploration, NPC interaction, and equipment management
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Positioning and action economy on grid-based battlefields balanced against growing enemy threat; resource management of limited allied roster across varied encounters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Commanding a hand-picked army of unique characters with distinct personalities and abilities, building them from early recruits into a cohesive force capable of defeating world-threatening evil
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate rejection of Fire Emblem's design philosophy in favor of Dragon Quest-inspired approach; no permanent unit death; player army gains experience even in defeat; emphasis on character personality and recruitment over permadeath stakes; grid-based tactical layer as enhancement to traditional JRPG formula rather than its replacement

difficulty.continues_system: No game over on army defeat; player can retry battles after losses with accumulated experience retained
difficulty.checkpointing: Battle-level checkpointing; defeat does not reset progress, only Max's death costs half player money
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No difficulty selection documented; player agency primarily through roster selection, class promotion timing (levels 10-20), equipment choices, and optional retreats via Egress spell

progression.upgrade_system: Character class promotion system: units promote between levels 10-20, resetting to level 1 but gaining stat increases and new abilities; equipment purchasing and swapping
progression.unlock_mechanism: Character recruitment through plot encounters and NPC dialogue; class promotions unlock at level thresholds; battle participation required for EXP gain and leveling
replayability_hooks: Multiple playable characters with distinct personalities encourage army composition experimentation
Class promotion timing creates build variation
Battle retry without penalty allows grinding for advantage

world_building_techniques: Character recruitment narrative: each allied unit has background and personality, though most contribute little to main plot
NPC dialogue in towns and exploration mode
Plot-driven encounters that introduce antagonists (Kane, Darksol, Dark Dragon)
World lore: Kingdom of Guardiana, land of Rune, Shining Path mythology

boss_design.philosophy: Hierarchical antagonist structure with multiple threat tiers: Kane (initial false leader), Ramladu (political authority), Darksol (true manipulator), Dark Dragon (final cosmic threat). Boss encounters integrated into tactical grid battles requiring both positioning and damage allocation strategy
notable_bosses: Kane: Commands Runefaust hordes, initially perceived as primary antagonist
King Ramladu: Revealed to be mind-controlled by Darksol
Darksol: Manipulative final antagonist, orchestrates Dark Dragon resurrection
Dark Dragon: Cosmic evil force sealed away using Chaos Breaker (merged light/dark sword)

faction_indicators: Shining Force: player-commanded multi-character tactical unit
Runefaust: enemy faction commanded by Kane under Darksol's control
Kingdom of Guardiana: political authority (King Ramladu) compromised by Darksol's influence
danger_signals: Dark Dragon resurrection attempt through Shining Path opening
Darksol's hidden control of political leadership
Escalating enemy threat requiring expanding Allied roster
environmental_storytelling: Towns and landmarks serve as navigation objectives and NPC interaction points
Kingdom geography (Guardiana, Rune) establishes world scale
Battle locations reflect strategic military engagement across contested territory
